pillars.title: What are we building?
pillars.prompt: Describe the core objective, target audience, and primary value of this feature.
pillars.guidance: Think about: Who is this for? What problem does it solve? What's the main benefit?
examples: A task management app for remote teams to coordinate work asynchronously
An AI-powered code review tool that catches bugs before deployment
A real-time collaboration whiteboard for distributed design teams

pillars.title: What are we including?
pillars.prompt: Define the exact boundaries of the MVP (Minimum Viable Product). What features MUST be in version 1.0?
pillars.guidance: Be specific and concrete. List actual features, not vague goals.
examples: Task CRUD operations (create, read, update, delete)

pillars.title: What are we excluding?
pillars.prompt: State what will NOT be built in this iteration. This prevents feature creep.
pillars.guidance: Be explicit about what's out of scope to manage expectations.

pillars.title: How might we build it?
pillars.prompt: Share initial thoughts on technical approach: APIs, libraries, database schemas, architecture patterns.
pillars.guidance: Don't overthink this - just capture your initial technical instincts.
examples: Frontend: React with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
Backend: Node.js with Express, PostgreSQL database
Authentication: JWT tokens with refresh mechanism
Deployment: Docker containers on AWS ECS

pillars.title: How do we know it works?
pillars.prompt: Define the exact conditions that must be met for this feature to be considered complete and testable.
pillars.guidance: Make criteria measurable and specific. Avoid vague statements.
examples: Users can create a task in under 2 seconds
All CRUD operations return responses in <200ms
95% of users can complete onboarding without help
Zero critical bugs in production for 7 days

pillars.title: What's the timeline?
pillars.prompt: Define the project timeline: milestones, phases, and target completion dates.
pillars.guidance: Be realistic. Include buffer time for unknowns. Break into phases if needed.
examples: Beta launch: Week 7, Full launch: Week 8

pillars.title: What resources do we need?
pillars.prompt: Identify the team, tools, budget, and other resources required for success.
pillars.guidance: Think about: people, infrastructure, third-party services, budget constraints.
examples: Team: 2 full-stack developers, 1 designer, 1 QA engineer
Tools: GitHub, Figma, Linear for project management
Time commitment: 40 hours/week per team member

meta.purpose: Structured feature planning framework with timeline and resource planning
